返回本批次

我戒酒一年了

I quit drinking for a year

dynomight.net 作者 webninja 分数 70 评论 87 2026/04/28 09:26
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 2025 年 1 月上旬，一位家庭朋友过来吃午饭。我作为助产士的众多乐趣之一就是对新年决心的热爱，所以我问她是否已经做出了任何决心。她说不，但提到她有一些亲戚正在做“潮湿的一月”。
  2. 如果您不知道，“戒酒一月”是许多人在一月份戒酒时面临的挑战。这些人正在做一种变体，即人们不是不喝酒，而是少喝酒。
  3. 出于某种原因，这触发了我。我心想：“你开玩笑吧？你一个月都戒不了酒？你知道那有多可悲吗？”然后，“操你！操你一月的潮湿！你知道吗，我要戒酒一年！”
  4. 需要明确的是，这些想法是针对我从未见过的人的。回想起来，我想知道我的情感上到底发生了什么。但我认真对待决议，所以我感到很投入。
  5. 现在时间已经过去 15 个月了，所以我将做出报告。

Original Summary

  1. In early January 2025, a family friend was over for lunch. One of my many guilty midwit pleasures is a love of New Year’s resolutions, so I asked her if she had made any. She said no, but mentioned that she had some relatives that were doing “damp January”.
  2. In case you’re not aware, Dry January is a challenge many people do to quit drinking alcohol during the month of January. These folks were doing a variant in which, instead of not drinking, one simply drinks less.
  3. For some reason, this triggered me. I thought, “Are you kidding? You can’t even stop drinking for a single month? Do you know how pathetic that is?” And then, “Fuck you! Fuck you for doing damp January! You know what, I’m going to stop drinking for a year!”
  4. To be clear, these thoughts were directed at people I’ve never even met. In retrospect, I wonder what was going on with me emotionally. But I take resolutions seriously, so I felt committed.
  5. We are now 15 months down the timeline, so I’ll make my report.

评论（默认中文）

anarazel 2026/04/28 09:51
几年前，在经历了一些（不相关的）健康问题后，我戒酒了。我不怀念葡萄酒、啤酒，它们在相对较短的时间后就停止了——就像作者一样。但有趣的是，我仍然很怀念漫长的一天后喝一杯好苏格兰威士忌的感觉。不是嗡嗡声，而是尖锐与有趣的味道混合在一起。

我的睡眠好多了。我真的没有意识到，酒精不仅仅影响我喝酒后的那一晚，甚至连接下来的一两个晚上都有影响。

I stopped drinking a few years back, after some (unrelated) health stuff. I don't miss wine, beer, that stopped - like for the author - after a relatively short amount of time. But interestingly I still really miss the feeling of a good scotch after a long day. Not being buzzed, but the sharpness mixed with interesting tastes.

My sleep has gotten so much better. I really didn't realize that alcohol didn't affect just the night after I had a drink, but even the next one or two nights..

在 HN 中查看
lfaw 2026/04/28 16:49
您尝试过散叶绿茶吗？普洱等发酵陈茶的风味非常复杂。咖啡因含量可能相当低，因此晚上饮用是安全的。发现它比品尝更有趣。葡萄酒。
Have you tried loose leaf green tea? Fermented and aged tea like pu'er has very complex flavors. The caffeine content can be quite low so its safe to drink in the evening. Found it to be even more fun and interesting than tasting e.g. wine.
在 HN 中查看
al_borland 2026/04/28 11:41
我已经多次长时间戒酒，几个月后我可以感觉到整体能量水平和睡眠有所不同。它似乎有一些很微妙但很明显的长尾效应。如果有人已经 3 个多月没有尝试过戒掉它，我建议进行一个小实验。

我的血液检查也改善了很多。事实上，我完全忘记了完成手术后我就停止喝酒了，所以当医生问我做了什么才能看到这样的改善时，我不知道。这段时间我的体重也增加了，所以酒精的负面影响比体重更大……尽管两者都不理想。

I’ve stopped drinking several times for longer stretches and I can feel a difference after a couple months in overall energy levels and sleep. It seems to have some pretty long tail effects that are subtle, but noticeable. If anyone hasn’t tried cutting it out for 3+ months, I would recommend running a little experiment.

My bloodwork improved a lot as well. I had actually completely forgot I stopped drinking when I had it done, so when the doctor asked what I did to see such an improvement, I had no idea. I had gained weight during this period as well, so the alcohol had a bigger negative impact than the weight… though neither is ideal.

在 HN 中查看
bkjelden 2026/04/28 10:36
有趣的是，在过去 18 个月左右的时间里，我尝试过戒酒/积极减少饮酒以及积极减少甜食（有时我非常专注，有时则不然）。

与OP的经验相反，我实际上发现糖果比酒精更有影响力。也就是说，晚餐后吃甜点对我睡眠的影响比喝一两杯还要大，而且我减肥的时期与我吃很少甜食的时期的相关性更为密切。

当然，对于这两种情况，低/中等消耗与零消耗基线之间的差异很小 - 过度消耗会带来麻烦。

和OP一样，几周后我发现日常仪式非常容易，但和OP一样，我也错过了社交方面（这就是为什么我认为完全戒酒是不值得的，至少对我来说）。如果我觉得我的生活质量正在经历阶跃功能改善，我会继续下去……但对我来说，这并不是它所证明的灵丹妙药。

我并不是真正将一种食品成分标记为所有健康问题的原因，但如果我必须选择一种，我会选择精制糖，而不是酒精。

Interesting, over the last 18 months or so I've dabbled with both quitting/aggressively cutting back on drinking as well as aggressively cutting back on sweets (some periods where I've been super dedicated and others where I'm not).

Counter to OP's experience, I've actually found sweets to be more impactful than alcohol. That is to say eating dessert after dinner will impact my sleep more than a drink or two, and the periods where I lost weight have been more closely correlated with the periods where I was eating very few sweets.

Of course with both, the delta between low/moderate consumption and a baseline of zero consumption is low - it's the excessive consumption that causes trouble.

Like OP I found the daily ritual to be pretty easy after a couple weeks, but like OP I also missed the social aspect (and this is why I've sort of settled on the opinion that giving up drinking completely is not worth it, at least for me). If I felt like I was experiencing a step function improvement in life quality I'd keep it up... but for me it just hasn't been the silver bullet it's made out to be.

I'm not really one to label one food ingredient as the cause of all health problems, but if I had to choose one I would choose refined sugar long before alcohol.

在 HN 中查看
techcode 2026/04/28 14:21
你的“甜食/甜点对我睡眠的影响比一两杯饮料更大”是如何/基于什么的？

我在闹钟响之前一个小时或更长时间就醒了（所以早上 7 点闹钟就在 <= 早上 6 点起床）。我认为我的睡眠很好 - 毕竟 Fitbit 睡眠得分为 80-85。

然后戒酒后我开始睡得更久。特别是起床较晚，至少在最初几周是这样。

由于皮质醇过早增加，酒精似乎导致了早醒。

而醒来时间需要几周的时间来重新校准。戒酒后 2-3 天 - Fitbit 的实际睡眠质量指标明显改善 - HRV 上升，RHR/BR 下降。

现在，我的“糟糕的夜晚”的 Fitbit 睡眠得分约为 85，而且通常为 90+。

实验室结果是白天和黑夜的 - 例如CRP 为 20（>5 表示炎症），戒酒几周后，当我感冒/发烧时，CRP 约为 10，现在 <1。

对我来说最大/最困难的问题是戒酒后，我的甜食摄入量增加了，尤其是在晚上。我白天几乎不吃碳水化合物，然后在晚上......我猜大脑缺乏一些简单的多巴胺，以前可以从酒精中获得......我会渴望糖果，冰淇淋......等。

花了近3个月的时间才能够坚持严格的生酮饮食。我最终摄入<=20克碳水化合物，因为从过去的经验来看——任何更高的碳水化合物摄入量我都很难限制在50克（这仍然是低碳水化合物/酮）。

How/what are you basing your "sweets/desert impacts my sleep more than a drink or two" on?

I was waking up an hour or more before the alarm (so waking up <=6AM with 7AM alarm). And I thought my sleep was good - after all Fitbit sleep score was 80-85.

Then after stopping alcohol I started sleeping longer. Specifically waking up later, at least for the first few weeks.

Seemingly alcohol was causing earlier waking due to spiking cortisol too early.

While waking-up time took a few weeks to recalibrate. Already 2-3 days after stopping alcohol - Fitbit was showing clear improvements in actual sleep quality metrics - HRV was increasing and RHR/BR were decreasing.

And now my "bad nights" have Fitbit sleep score of ~85, and it's regularly 90+.

Lab results are night and day - e.g. CRP was 20 (>5 signals inflammation), just few weeks after stopping alcohol it was ~10 while I was having cold/fever, and now it's <1.

The biggest/hardest problem for me was that after stopping alcohol, my sweets intake increased, especially in the evening. I was doing almost no carbs during the day, and then in the evening ... I guess brain was lacking some easy dopamine that it would previously get from alcohol... I would crave sweets, ice cream ...etc.

It took almost 3 months to be able to stick with strict keto diet. I'm finally doing <=20 gram of carbs because from past experience - any higher and I have a hard time limiting carbs to say just 50 (which would still be low carb/keto).

browningstreet 2026/04/28 10:12
我记得一位酒吧熟人戒酒一年了。那一年结束时我见到了他，我问他过得怎么样。

“我没有注意到任何变化，所以我要再辞职一年。”

I remember a bar acquaintance who stopped drinking for a year. I saw him when that year was up and I asked him how it went.

“I didn’t notice any changes, so I’m quitting for another year.”

在 HN 中查看
steve1977 2026/04/28 12:28
我就是这么做的。由于一些健康原因，我最初停止了一个月。又过了一个月。还有另一个。现在已经4年多一点了。

有趣的是，当我戒烟时（差不多20年前），我知道我绝对不能再碰香烟了。我想对于酒精来说，我可以偶尔喝点葡萄酒或啤酒，只是我觉得不再需要了。

That's kind of what I did. I initially stopped for a month for some health reasons. That became another month. And another one. And now it's a bit more than 4 years.

The interesting thing is, when I quit smoking (almost 20 years ago), I knew I must never touch a cigarette again. I think with alcohol, I could have some wine or beer occassionaly, I just don't feel the need for it anymore.

在 HN 中查看
ffsm8 2026/04/28 11:55
TBF，如果你没有喝太多（例如每周或每月一次）适量 - 所以不足以喝醉......那么你的身体能够处理这个问题。

从统计数据来看，你患癌症等的可能性仍然更大，但这仍然是一个相当低的可能性，因此......完全禁欲可能是最健康的，但也是不必要的。

Tbf, if you're not drinking much (eg only once per week or month) a moderate amount - so not enough to get drunk... Then your body is able to handle that.

Statistically you're still more likely to get cancer etc, but that's still a pretty low likelihood, hence... Complete abstinence may be healthiest, but is also unnecessary.

在 HN 中查看
loveparade 2026/04/28 09:55
我已经有六个月左右没有喝过酒了。不是因为我想停下来，而是我最近没有喝酒的欲望。

现在我很想告诉你随之而来的所有令人惊奇的神奇健康益处，但不幸的是没有。我觉得完全没有区别。

I haven't had a drink in 6 months or so. Not because I wanted to stop, I just have not had the desire to drink recently.

Now I would love to tell you about all the amazing magical healthy benefits that have come with that, but unfortunately there are none. I feel no difference at all.

Eddy_Viscosity2 2026/04/28 10:25
当我停止饮酒时，我几乎立即减掉了 15 磅。几周后它就掉下来了，没有做任何其他不同的事情。这只是饮料本身和我通常在喝酒时或喝酒后吃的零食带来的额外卡路里。它也保持关闭状态。
When I stopped drinking I almost immediately lost like 15 lbs. It just fell off without doing anything else different over a few weeks. It was just the extra calories from both the drinks themselves and the snacks I would usually eat with or after drinks. It stayed off too.

Based from my experience, stopping drinking didn't have noticeable effects as well. However, whenever I would drink once in a blue moon socially, it feels even worse than what I remember. I think it just felt normal before.

californical 2026/04/28 10:19
但我敢打赌，每当（如果？）你再次喝一两杯啤酒时，你都会注意到它如何影响你的睡眠，并且可能只会让你感觉有点无聊。

我是作为一个偶尔喝酒的人这么说的。我的一些家人几乎每天晚上都喜欢喝酒。但我偶尔会加入他们，只是感觉有点臃肿和糟糕，尤其是几周之后。使做出其他健康选择变得更加困难

但是，是的，我喜欢啤酒的味道，所以这都是关于权衡的！

But I bet whenever (if?) you do have a beer or two again, you’ll notice how it affects your sleep and probably just makes you feel kinda meh.

I say that as an occasional drinker. Some of my family loves to drink, nearly every evening. But I join them occasionally and just feel kinda bloated and bad, especially if it’s been a few weeks. Makes it harder to make other healthy choices

But yeah love the taste of beer so it is all about tradeoffs!

在 HN 中查看
zeroq 2026/04/28 10:35
当你在社交场合戒酒时，戒酒很容易。

有很多人有意识地想戒酒，但找不到其他方法来阻止他们头脑中的风暴。这可能是由多种原因引起的，从创伤到多动症。

我听到的关于成瘾的最好的名言是：“我只能控制我的第一杯酒”。

最糟糕的是，酒精和毒品具有强烈的耻辱感，但对于正在遭受痛苦的人来说，任何可以让他们失去理智的事情都是可行的，赌博、狂看电视或玩电子游戏。后者常常被亲戚忽视和忽视。

It's easy to stop drinking when you do it socially.

There are many people who would consciously love to stop drinking but can't find alternative to stop the storm in their heads. This could be caused by many things, from trauma to ADHD.

The best quote I heard about addiction is: "I only have control over my first drink".

The worst part is that alcohol and drugs have a strong stigma, but for people who are suffering anything that can turn their mind off is viable, gambling, binge watching tv or playing video games. The latter are often overlooked and ignored by relatives.

not_a_bot_4sho 2026/04/28 10:31
不要喝酒。或者如果必须的话，少喝一点。

我是谁来告诉你该怎么做？没有一个重要的人。但我最近确实发现自己患有结肠癌，也许与我对啤酒的喜爱有关。现在我正在尴尬地思考我的最终退出策略。 0/5 星不推荐。

哦，还有安排一下你一直推迟的结肠镜检查。当有更多治疗选择时，最好尽早发现。

Don't drink. Or if you must, drink less.

Who am I to tell you what to do? No one important. But I did recently discover I have colon cancer, perhaps related to my great fondness for beer. And now I'm awkwardly figuring out my final exit strategy. 0/5 stars not recommended.

Oh, and schedule that colonoscopy you've been putting off. Better to catch it early when more treatment options are on the table.

在 HN 中查看
SapporoChris 2026/04/28 11:09
我查了结直肠癌的危险因素，发现它受到各种生活方式和遗传因素的影响，包括：饮食（大量红肉和加工肉类）、肥胖、吸烟和缺乏体力活动。

我还注意到，利率似乎实际上正在下降。至少根据国家癌症研究所的说法是这样。 https://seer.cancer.gov/statfacts/html/colorect.html

I looked up the risk factors for Colorectal cancer and it is influenced by various lifestyle and genetic factors, including: Diet (high in red and processed meats), Obesity, Smoking, and Lack of physical activity.

I also noted that rates are appear to actually be decreasing. At least according to the National Cancer Institute. https://seer.cancer.gov/statfacts/html/colorect.html

是的，人们吸烟越来越少了。
Yes, people are smoking less.
在 HN 中查看
HerbManic 2026/04/28 10:34
我会支持早期测试，它在早期就发现了我母亲的癌症。那是10年前的事了，她已经70多岁了，身体很强壮。

至于最终的退出策略，做对你有用的事情，不要担心别人认为你“自私”。你是最终的仲裁者。

祝你一切顺利。

I will second the early testing, it caught my mothers cancer in the early stages. That was 10 years ago and she is going strong in her mid 70's.

As for the final exit strategy, do what works for you and don't worry about others thinking you are being "selfish". You are the final arbiter.

Be well.

在 HN 中查看
irishcoffee 2026/04/28 10:49
在我 30 多岁的时候（我现在年纪大了），我得了乳腺癌，我喝了很多酒，那是在我得了胰腺炎之后，因为我太他妈愚蠢了，无法吸取教训。我是一个生理上的男性，现在距离一组还差一个乳头。

哦，还有酒后驾车。睡在行驶的汽车里。我是个蠢货，也是个白痴。

我喝完了。理想情况下，我的脚不会那么麻木和疼痛，我的肩膀可能会停止疼痛，我左手的外侧两个手指可能会变得不麻木，甚至可能会增加一些重量。

我的家人都是一群酗酒者，如果你相信这种事情的话，他们是“功能性的”。我意识到，如果不出意外的话，我需要为我的孩子打破这个循环。

得知您的癌症诊断结果，我感到非常遗憾。我当时（现在）都很害怕，因为到目前为止我的生命已经缩短了大约 20 年，而且现在任何一年都可能结束。

酗酒可不是开玩笑。我刚刚给自己写了一封 17k 的信（我的配偶作为观众，不确定我是否会向她展示，她现在讨厌我）关于我是如何到达现在的位置的。读回来并编辑它让我哭了好几个小时。我不敢相信这个帖子突然出现了。

In my mid 30s (I'm older now) I got breast cancer I drank so much, which was after I got pancreatitis because I'm too fucking stupid to learn my lesson. I am a biological male now one nipple shy of a set.

Oh and a DUI. Sleeping in a running car. I’m a jackass and an idiot.

I'm done drinking. Ideally my feet will be less numb and painful, my shoulder might stop hurting, the outer two fingers on my left hand might become un-numb, might even gain some weight.

My family is a bunch of alcoholics, "functional" if you believe in that sort of thing. I realized if nothing else, I need to break that cycle for my kids.

I'm so sorry to hear about your cancer diagnosis. I was (and am) terrified that I took ~20 years off my life up to this point, and it could be over any year now.

Alcoholism is no joke. I just wrote up a 17k letter to myself (with my spouse as the audience, not sure I'll ever show her, she hates me right now) about how I got to where I am. Reading it back and editing it has had me bawling for hours. I can't believe this thread popped up.

tim-tday 2026/04/28 10:55
我认为一致性是王道。今天早上我只是在想，如果过去 20 年来我在酒吧喝的每一杯酒都进入股市，我可能会变得更富有 100 万美元。我二十多岁的时候没有在股市上投入任何资金，因为我“买不起”，但不知怎的，我有能力出去每晚花一百美元买酒。 然后是宿醉，很肯定每一次严重的宿醉都会让我永远变得更愚蠢。 如果我能回到过去并踢自己一脚，我会的。
Consistency is king I suppose. I was just thinking this morning about how if every drink I had at a bar over the last 20 years had gone into the stock market I’d probably be a million dollars richer. I was putting nothing into the stock market in my twenties because I “couldn’t afford it” but somehow I could afford to go out and spend a hundred bucks a night on booze. Then there’s the hangovers, pretty sure every bad hangover made me permanently stupider. If I could go back in time and kick myself I would.

$100/night in 2006? Wow.. NYC? That is a lot of income that could be disposed of elsewhere.

x______________ 2026/04/28 13:38

在 HN 中查看
dhruvkar 2026/04/28 10:08
12 年里，我一年工作，一年休息（2012 年 - 2024 年）。

在我的ON岁月里，我觉得有必要喝酒，每天晚上我至少喝一杯啤酒，如果没有其他原因，我想“充分利用它”。

在我休息的那几年，我不觉得需要喝酒，而且通常睡得更好，体重减轻，注意力更集中。

之后就完全戒酒了。

For 12 years, I did one year ON, one year OFF (2012 - 2024).

On my ON years, I felt compelled to drink, every night I had at least a beer, if for no reason other than, I wanted to make the "most of it".

On my OFF years, I didn't feel the need to drink, and generally slept better, lost weight and was more focused.

Stopped drinking completely after that.

在 HN 中查看
senkora 2026/04/28 10:44
我赞扬您对统计严谨性的承诺。

与此相关的是，“序贯分析”是实验设计统计数据中的关键词，允许在出现明确结果时尽早停止实验： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sequential_analysis#

I applaud your commitment to statistical rigor.

On a related note, “sequential analysis” is the keyword in stats for experimental designs that allow for stopping the experiment early in the face of clear results: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sequential_analysis#

dhruvkar 2026/04/28 11:18

haha... I'm slow..needed the dozen years of data.
在 HN 中查看
chrysoprace 2026/04/28 10:11
我发现现在我很喜欢它的味道（这常常让不喝啤酒的人感到惊讶），所以我很高兴非酒精饮料的选择变得越来越普遍。

我的主要愿望是让不含酒精的精酿啤酒变得更普及、更便宜。在澳大利亚，酒精饮料的税率高得离谱，但非酒精饮料则不然，但它们的价格往往相同——这令人失望。

I've found that nowadays I largely like it for the taste (which often surprises non-beer drinkers) and so I'm quite pleased that non-alcoholic options are becoming more widespread.

My main wish is for non-alcoholic craft beer to become much more widespread and cheaper. In Australia alcohol is taxed at a ludicrous rate, but non-alcoholic drinks are not, however they often attract the same price - which is disappointing.

在 HN 中查看
boyter 2026/04/28 10:21
如果酒有同样的味道我会同意的。没有一种无酒精的葡萄酒味道接近，而且它们本身都不好。

有些不含酒精的啤酒非常好喝，我很乐意喝。

Id be ok with that if wine had the same taste. No alcohol free wine tastes even close, and none of them are good in their own right.

Some of the non-alcoholic beers are pretty good though and I am happy to drink them.

nunez 2026/04/28 14:01
我迫不及待地想要一款真正可以饮用的任何年份的北美葡萄酒。我的最后一个太可怕了。

和我喝的 NA 威士忌一样。

然而，假冒的内格罗尼却相当可靠。

i cannot wait for an actually drinkable NA wine of any vintage. the last one i had was horrible.

same with the NA whiskey i had.

phony negroni, however, is pretty solid.

在 HN 中查看
esafak 2026/04/28 10:15
令我烦恼的一件事是，在美国，无酒精啤酒需要接受年龄检查。我认为这是因为程序员没有为他们创建一个单独的组，所以他们用剩下的酒打电话，触发检查？也许有一个很好的理由（因为这不是一个）。
One thing that annoys me is that non-alcoholic beer is subject to age checks in the US. I assume it's because the programmers didn't create a separate group for them, so they ring up with the rest of the liquor, triggering a check? Maybe there's a good reason ('coz that's not one).
在 HN 中查看
mk89 2026/04/28 10:26
很可能是因为即使是不含酒精的啤酒也仍然含有大约 0.5% 的酒精。

除非它是“0.0%”的无酒精啤酒，即便如此，它仍然可能含有一点……

Most likely because even non-alcoholic beer still contains like 0.5% of alcohol.

Unless it's a "0.0%" alcohol-free beer, and even then it might still contain a bit...

在 HN 中查看
zdragnar 2026/04/28 10:35
我一时兴起从杂货店的啤酒冷却器里买了啤酒花味的苏打水，尽管我试图向可怜的收银员解释其中的逻辑，但我们都屈服于计算机系统的突发奇想，扫描了我的身份证件以完成购买。

还有其他饮料含有微量酒精，例如康普茶，其酒精含量被限制在 0.5% 以下。果汁也可能含有相同数量的以上，具体取决于加工量。

I bought hops-flavored sparkling water from a grocery store's beer cooler on a lark and, despite my attempt to explain the logic to the poor cashier, we both bent to the whims of the computer system and scanned my ID to complete the purchase.

There are other drinks that have trace amounts of alcohol, such as kombucha which is regulated to stay under the 0.5% threshold. Fruit juices will also likely contain upwards of the same amount, depending on how much they're processed.

I think orange juice can be up to 0.5% alcohol, and they don't check ID for that.

在 HN 中查看
nunez 2026/04/28 13:51
> 我确实发现不喝酒有一个明显的缺点：如果你不喝酒，那么和喝酒的人出去玩就没那么有趣了

这是最重要的。酒精是许多社交活动的润滑剂，无论是好是坏。我已将酒精摄入量减少至每次 2 杯，每小时摄入量约为 1 杯（随着 ABV 的增加而减慢）。这样做的一大好处是，在外面待到很晚并不那么有吸引力。

不管怎样，这篇文章读起来就像我戒掉含咖啡因咖啡的那一年。休斯敦的一家烘焙店有一种屡获殊荣的无咖啡因咖啡豆，它令人难以置信，足以让我当场做出决定。事实证明，他们用那颗豆子赢得了 WBC 冠军是有原因的！假设商店甚至这样做的话，要喝一杯好的无咖啡因手冲咖啡是非常困难的。大多数时候，你只能喝陈旧的浓缩咖啡，或者什么都不喝。

我已将其重新引入我的饮食中，但每周都有无咖啡因的日子，以便咖啡因在我需要时继续“发挥作用”。

> I did find not drinking to have one clear downside: It’s just not that much fun to hang out with people who are drinking if you are not drinking yours

This is the big one. Alcohol is the lubricant that makes many social activities function, for better or worse. I've reduced my alcohol intake to 2 drinks per session with about a drink per hour intake rate (slower as ABV increases). One big plus to doing this is that staying out super late isn't nearly as appealing.

Regardless, this article reads like the year I quit drinking caffineated coffee. A roaster here in Houston had an award winning decaf bean that was incredible enough for me to make that decision on the spot. It turns out that they won the WBC on that bean for a reason! It is outstandingly hard to get a good decaf pourover, assuming that the shop even does that. Most times, you're stuck with old espresso or nothing, more often than not.

I've introduced it back into my diet but have decaf days throughout the week so that caffeiene continues to "work" when I need it.

在 HN 中查看
anonymousiam 2026/04/28 12:35
我来自一个酗酒的家庭（我的一半叔叔阿姨、我的父亲和我的妹妹），我亲眼目睹了酒精对社会和身体造成的损害。 我的父亲是我不该成为的榜样。 我确实喝酒，但没有什么比酒更烈的了，而且也不经常喝酒。

对于作者来说，戒掉可能很容易，但对于上瘾的人来说，情况就不同了。 那些试图戒烟的人至少意识到他们遇到了问题。 许多人甚至从未走到那么远。

Coming from a family of alcoholics (half of my aunts and uncles, my father, and my sister), I have seen the social and physical damage that alcohol does. My father was a role model to me of what not to become. I do drink, but nothing stronger than wine, and not very often.

It may have been easy for the author to quit, but it's a different story for the people who are addicted. The ones who are trying to quit are at least aware that they've got a problem. Many never even make it that far.

在 HN 中查看
charles_f 2026/04/28 10:05
这很鼓舞人心，你可能说服我也这样做，哪怕只是为了睡眠部分。

我想念的一件事是味道，我不喝苏打水或含糖饮料，而且我想不出有什么可以替代优质葡萄酒、啤酒或烈酒的味道。 （现在有很好的脱冷啤酒，但感觉像是作弊）。我想并不是说人们绝对需要它，但它仍然是烹饪体验的一部分。可能是因为我从来没有看过，有什么“大人”喝的非酒精饮料吗？

This is inspiring, you might have convinced me to do the same, if only for the sleep part.

One thing that I'd miss is the taste though, I don't drink soda or sugary drinks, and I can't think of a replacement for the taste of good wine, beer or spirits. (there's good dealcoolized beers nowadays but it feels like cheating). Not that I guess one absolutely needs it, but is still part of the culinary experience. It's probably because I never looked though, is there anything non alcoholic that "grown ups" drink?

在 HN 中查看
aardvarkdriver 2026/04/28 10:57
第二杯咖啡和茶。我已经快一年了，很喜欢品尝和学习茶的起源和加工方法，就像人们学习精酿啤酒一样。同样令人满意，缺点也更少。

另外，我不会认为 NA 啤酒作弊！对我来说，社交饮酒的仪式和味道一样重要。和朋友一起喝上好的 NA 啤酒是 95% 的解决方案。

Second coffee and tea. I'm nearing a year and have enjoyed tasting and learning about origins and processing methods of tea in a similar way one might for do craft beer. Just as fulfilling with less downside.

Also, I wouldn't consider NA beer cheating! For me, the ritual of social drinking was just as important as the taste. Drinking good NA beers with friends is a 95% solution.

californical 2026/04/28 10:21
咖啡和茶！晚上的洋甘菊很美，总是让我感觉很舒服
Coffee and tea! Chamomile is nice in the evening, always makes me feel cozy
在 HN 中查看
budududuroiu 2026/04/28 10:00
2025年我有8个月没有喝酒，然后我去东欧参加朋友的婚礼，那里预计会喝酒。经过大约两个月的酗酒之后，我又回到了不喝酒的火车上。

我想这次让我坚持下来的是骑自行车。如果我想起床上路，即使是前一天晚上喝一杯也会完全破坏我的计划，所以计算完全改变了。

喝酒真的很有趣，在早上七点之前喝酒真的很有趣，但现在我更喜欢清晨喝咖啡和骑自行车。

I didn't drink for 8 months in 2025, then I went to friends' weddings in Eastern Europe where it's expected to drink. After about 2 months of binging, I'm back on the no-drinking train.

I think what made it stick for me this time is cycling. If I want to be up and hit the road, even one drink the night before will totally derail that, so the calculation completely shifted.

Drinking is really fun, being up to no good until 7am on a bender is really fun, but I much prefer my early mornings with a coffee and cycling nowadays.

在 HN 中查看
paologiacometti 2026/04/28 14:52
经过一月的干燥年份后，我从 20 岁到 40 岁酗酒，周末只喝一杯啤酒或几杯酒（无一例外），我的整体能量和睡眠增加了一个数量级。我明年的目标就是辞职。戒烟后，这是我迄今为止最好的健康决定之一
Heavy drinker from 20 to 40 now after some dry January years I only drink a beer or a couple of wine glass in the weekends (no exception) my overall energy and sleep increased by an order of magnitude. I will quit as a target next year. After quit smoking one of my best health decision so far

pton_xd 2026/04/28 09:51

Whoa! That's not healthy at all... ;)

I've found non-alcoholic beers are actually pretty tasty these days, moreso the longer its been since having a real beer. They definitely scratch the itch to "have something" while out socializing. I don't miss the alcohol at all.

在 HN 中查看
friggeri 2026/04/28 10:37
我八九年前就戒酒了，而且一直没有怀念。这并不是一个问题，我时不时地喜欢喝一杯葡萄酒或鸡尾酒，但我正在为超级马拉松进行训练，并且认为我会在训练几个月时保持干燥。比赛几天后喝了一杯酒，感觉彻底崩溃了，此时我意识到如果我想再次喝酒，我必须“训练”我的身体，并认为这完全是倒退。我还没有发现任何情况下酒精饮料不能被一杯水、一杯啤酒或一杯无酒精鸡尾酒代替。我也很幸运能嫁给一个出色的女人，她得出了或多或少相同的结论，我们喜欢成为不喝酒的无聊人（但永远不会宿醉）。
I stopped drinking 8 or 9 years ago, and I haven’t missed it. Not that it ever was a problem, I enjoyed a glass of wine or a cocktail every now and then, but I was training for an ultra marathon and figured I’d stay dry while I trained for a couple of months. Had a glass of wine a few days after the race and felt completely hammered, at which point I realized if I wanted to drink again I’d have to “train” my body for it, and thought that was totally backwards. I haven’t found any situation where an alcoholic drink can’t be replaced by a glass of water, an NA beer or a mocktail. I’m also very lucky to be married to a wonderful woman who arrived to more or less the same conclusion and we enjoy being the boring ones who don’t drink (but never get a hangover).

The author had 2-3 drinks per week, that really doesn’t sound like “drinking”. Maybe I’m the one who should give it up.

HerbManic 2026/04/28 10:33
也许这只是我的问题，但我在规范此类东西的消费方面从未遇到过任何重大问题？酒精固然是一件好事，但如果你愿意的话，它永远不会成为一种习惯或饮食。即使当我发现我对乳制品过敏时，扔掉所有牛奶/奶酪之类的东西也只是记住要做的事情，没有持久的渴望。

但是咖啡因……天哪，这可太难了。

Maybe it is just me but I had never had any major issues in regulating consumption of stuff like this? Alcohol is all well a good but it never became a habit or diet if you will. Even when I found out I had a dairy allergy, dropping all milks/cheese and whatnot was just a case of remembering to do it, no lasting desire for it.

But Caffeine... oh man that is a tough one.

在 HN 中查看
Ronsenshi 2026/04/28 11:27
这个评论区总体来说很奇怪。很少有人评论说他们似乎没有控制饮酒的问题，他们可以享受一杯而不成为问题。

当然，有问题的人能够认识到它并阻止它影响他们的生活当然是件好事，但我觉得必须有更多的人完全可以接受不规律地少量饮酒作为社交活动的一部分。

This comments section is bizarre in general. Very few comments from people who don't seem to have an issue with control over their alcohol consumption where they can enjoy a glass without it becoming a problem.

Naturally it's good that people who do have a problem can recognize it and stop it from affecting their life, but I feel like there has to be more people who are perfectly OK with irregular consumption of light amounts of alcohol as part of a social activity.

在 HN 中查看
littlexsparkee 2026/04/28 10:25
我太喜欢啤酒了，无法放弃啤酒，但我一次只喝 1-2 杯，很少 - 但不喝烈酒、葡萄酒等也没有问题。除非有人给我提供一些东西，否则我会忘记后者的存在。如果没有啤酒，你不会找到像地狱啤酒、马森啤酒、凯勒啤酒这样不太常见的啤酒，所以在我看来，它并不是啤酒爱好者的真正替代品。
I love lagers too much to give up beer but I only have 1-2 at a time, quite rarely - but doing without spirits, wine, etc is no problem. The latter I forget exist unless someone else offers me something. With n/a beer, you're not going to find less common types like helles, marzen, kellerbier so it's not a true replacement for aficionados imo.

cokerocks 2026/04/28 10:38
等等，人们一月潮湿吗？

我一月和九月不喝苏打水已经有 15 年了，这是为了避免肾结石，我家里有很多肾结石病例。

每个人都告诉这个反应，好像这是一个新奇的概念，并想到这样做是为了吸烟或喝咖啡。我以前从未听说过潮湿的一月。

Wait, people do damp januaries?

I have been doing no-soda januaries and septembers for 15 years now, as a way to avoid kidney stones, something my family has a number of cases of.

Everyone told this to reacted as if it was a novel concept and thought of doing it for smoking or coffee. I never heard of damp januaries before.

我戒酒九年了，又重新开始喝酒。我现在每隔几个月就会这样做一次，只是为了和老朋友喝一三杯啤酒。但我并没有被拉回来，事实上，我已经有两个月没有再喝酒了。只是必须学会在没有它的情况下生活。
I quit for 9 years and went back to drinking. I do it once every few months now, just to have a beer or three with old friends. But I didn't get dragged back in, in fact, it's been 2 months since I drank again. Just had to learn to live my life without it.

I don't drink regularly at all, never have.

This means that at most, I tend to consume no more than 1-2 drinks per year, usually if I'm curious about how a particular beverage tastes and there's no alternative drinks available to have with my meal.

anal_reactor 2026/04/28 14:43
人生苦短，吸毒吧。我戒掉所有药物三个月了，没有做任何事。我计划在秋天去看精神科医生，治疗我的慢性疲劳和睡眠问题，以防万一我开出一些不与物质结合的药物，今年夏天我会狂吃大麻和酸，因为他妈的清醒。

> 看来不用酒精也能重现这种效果。

这是你必须训练的技能。通常，当团队中只有一个人调节气氛，而其他人将该人视为领导者时，他们就会跟随。观察醉酒的人的行为，并在遇到朋友时模仿这一点。我和同事大多是朋友，所以午餐时我们会谈论有趣的事情，而首席执行官上方的两张桌子正在做有关人工智能的讲座。

Life is short, do drugs. I quit all substances for three months, didn't do jack shit. I'm planning to see a psychiatrist in autumn for my chronic exhaustion and sleep problems, and just in case I get prescribed something that doesn't combine with substances, this summer I'm binging on weed and acid because fuck being sober.

> It seems like it should be possible to reproduce this effect without alcohol.

It's a skill you have to train. Usually when you have one person in a group who sets the mood, and the rest see that person as a leader, they will follow. Observe how drunk people behave and replicate this whenever you meet your friends. I'm mostly friends with my coworkers so during lunch we talk about fun stuff while two tables over the CEO is giving a lecture about AI.

2Gkashmiri 2026/04/28 09:56
我一直试图向人们询问这个问题，但尚未得到满意的答案。

当然，酒精在社会中根深蒂固，美国人也尝试过禁酒等等，但这并不是悲观和厄运。 大约 20 亿穆斯林选择不饮酒并过着戒酒的生活。

没有它他们也能过得很好。这对他们有什么作用？ ？

我的意思是我从来没有消费过，也永远不会消费，那么为什么你们的社会认为它可以接受呢？

托巴博的消费量不是因为被征税而下降吗？

为什么你不能自愿尝试通过缴纳“酒精税”来影响你的饮酒量？

不喝酒有好处，也有与酒精相关的问题，那些试图说服酒精对你有好处的人通常是在欺骗自己。

I have been trying to ask people this but its havent gotten a satisfactory answer.

Sure alcohol is ingrained in society, sure Americans tried prohibition and all that but its not gloom and doom. Muslims who are about 2 billion choose not to consume alcohol and go about their lives without it.

They just do fine without it. How does that work for them? ?

I mean I have never consumed and never will so why is it that your society finds it acceptable?

Isnt tobabbo going down in consumption because it is being taxed shit out of?

Why can't you voluntarily try to influence your alcohol consumption by paying a "alcohol tax"?

There are benefits of not consuming alcohol, there are problems associated with alcohol, and people who try to convince alcohol is good for you are generally lying to themselves.

在 HN 中查看
defrost 2026/04/28 10:18
这些都不是很好的“结构良好”的问题，也许需要努力提高它们。

> 我的意思是我从来没有消费过，也永远不会消费，那么为什么你们的社会认为它可以接受呢？

好吧，地球上有许多社会，一般来说，他们的行为和规范并不取决于你的个人选择和决定。

例如：我的父亲是一位功勋卓著的退伍军人，90 年来一直是终身滴酒不沾，澳大利亚社会在很大程度上对此漠不关心。

> 烟草的消费量不是因为被征税而下降吗？

是/否/并非如此——事情比这更复杂。澳大利亚的经验是，公共卫生教育政策促进健康、烟草的缺点以及要求香烟包装上显示患病器官的图形图像 耦合的 随着烟草消费税的增加，共同减少烟草使用...... 但 ..

最终，随着烟草税的持续增加（恕我直言，这可能是一个坏主意），犯罪分子回避和黑市烟草的动机增加，澳大利亚现在出现了一个比以前更加暴力的新犯罪阶层。与此同时，电子烟在某种程度上回避了与香烟的联系，年轻人又重新将这些“禁止”的东西视为可取的。

> 为什么你不能自愿尝试通过缴纳“酒精税”来影响你的饮酒量？

我猜假设“你”指的是一个影响其人口的国家？

许多国家都有酒精税，还有对酒后驾驶的处罚、对工作时醉酒的商业限制等。

澳大利亚人整体上饮酒量比许多非澳大利亚人想象的要少——当然有人喝酒很多，但人均饮酒量不像以前那么多，人均饮酒量也不像以前那么多，而且他们确实喜欢渲染有关澳大利亚的神话。

These aren't great "well formed" questions, maybe work on sharpening them up.

> I mean I have never consumed and never will so why is it that your society finds it acceptable?

Well, there are many societies across the planet and as a general rule their behaviours and norms are not predicated upon your personal choices and decisions.

eg: Australian society is largely indifferent to the fact that my father, a decorated service veteran, is and has been a lifelong teetotaller for 90 years.

> Isn't tobacco usage going down in consumption because it is being taxed shit out of?

Yes / no / not really - it's more complex than that. The Australian experience is that public health education policy promoting health, the downsides of tobacco, and requiring cigarette packs to display graphic images of diseased organs coupled with increased tobacco consumption taxes worked together to reduce tobacco use ... But ..

eventually, as tobacco taxes continued to increase (probably a bad idea IMHO) the incentives for criminal sidestepping and black market tobacco increased and Australia now has a new class of criminals that are considerably more violent than before. Hand in hand with that vaping has somehow sidestepped being associated with ciggies, and younger people are back to seeing these 'forbidden' things as desirable.

> Why can't you voluntarily try to influence your alcohol consumption by paying a "alcohol tax"?

Assuming by "you" you mean a country influencing it's population, I guess?

There are alcohol taxes in many countries, also penalties for drink driving, business restrictions on being drunk at work, etc.

Australians as a whole consume less alcohol than many non Australians imagine - sure there are people that drink a lot, but not as many (per capita) as once were, nor as much volume wise per person as they used to, and they do enjoy playing up myths about Australia.

在 HN 中查看
TFNA 2026/04/28 10:38
穆斯林世界有大量饮酒者。一方面，有些国家以穆斯林为主，但酒精可以合法销售并广泛消费，例如巴尔干地区、中亚或西非部分地区。另一方面，像沙特阿拉伯和伊朗这样的国家，非法自制和分销（通常是非常肮脏的东西）盛行，但社会在保密方面做得很好。
The Muslim world has plenty of alcohol drinking. On one hand you have countries that are majority Muslim but alcohol is legally sold and widely consumed, like the Balkans, Central Asia or parts of West Africa. On the other hand you have countries like Saudi Arabia and Iran where illegal homebrewing and distribution (of often very foul stuff) is rife but society does a very good job of keeping it under wraps.
在 HN 中查看
sometimes_all 2026/04/28 13:19
> 某些美国人尝试过禁令和所有这些，但它并不是悲观和厄运

> 为什么你不能自愿尝试通过缴纳“酒精税”来影响你的饮酒量？

印度的古吉拉特邦是一个干旱的邦。 UP 的酒精税为 69%。根本没有阻止人们，事实上，这样的规则适得其反——漏洞、酒精旅游、非法生产造成各种破坏。

> 我的意思是我从来没有消费过，也永远不会消费，那么为什么你们的社会认为它可以接受呢？

我也没有。但问题恰恰出在你要去的地方。在印度，禁酒主义被认为是理想的，而在西方世界，饮酒被认为更容易被接受。在印度，我喝酒的压力要大得多。有趣的是，越是“禁忌”或“社会不可接受”的事情，参与的人就越强迫你去做。

> sure Americans tried prohibition and all that but its not gloom and doom

> Why can't you voluntarily try to influence your alcohol consumption by paying a "alcohol tax"?

Gujarat in India is a dry state. UP has an alcohol tax of 69%. Hasn't stopped people at all, in fact such rules have backfired - loopholes, alcohol tourism, illicit production causing all sorts of havoc.

> I mean I have never consumed and never will so why is it that your society finds it acceptable?

Neither have I. But the problem lies exactly where you're heading. I've had a ton more pressure to drink in India where teetotalism is considered ideal, than in the western world where drinking is considered a lot more acceptable. Funnily enough, the more "taboo" or "socially less acceptable" something is, the more the people who partake also force you to do it.

在 HN 中查看
jeremyjh 2026/04/28 10:15
许多州对酒精征税。酒精的受欢迎程度正在下降，但我认为这主要是由于大麻和四氢大麻酚合法化。事实上，现在因 THC 导致的酒后驾车比酒精还要多。

人们饮酒已有数千年历史，但一直以来，一直有人认为这是个坏主意。

就我个人而言，我无法成功饮酒，所以大约 17 年前我完全戒掉了。我唯一真正怀念的是偶尔和同事一起喝酒，尤其是在漫长的晚餐上。在餐厅里与喝得酩酊大醉的同事共进一顿漫长的晚餐是一种特殊的地狱。

A lot of states do tax alcohol. Alcohol is dropping in popularity, but I think that is mostly due to legalized Marijuana and THC. Actually, there are more DUI for THC now than Alcohol.

People have been drinking for thousands of years, and all that time there have been people who think its a bad idea.

For me personally I can't drink successfully, so I quit entirely about 17 years ago. The only thing I really miss is the occasional drink with co-workers, especially at long dinners. A long dinner at a restaurant - sober - with co-workers who are all drinking is a special kind of hell.

在 HN 中查看
adgjlsfhk1 2026/04/28 10:21
请注意，许多（大多数？） THC 酒后驾车几乎没有证据。 thc 会在你的系统中保留至少一个月，所以很难判断其中有多少是真正的酒驾，还是警察决定将一个人（通常是黑人）靠边停车并编造一些标志来证明逮捕+药物测试的合理性
note that many (most?) THC DUIs have very little evidence. thc stays in your system for at least a month, so it's very hard to tell how many of these are actual DUIs vs cops deciding to pull over a (usually black) person and make up some signs to justify an arrest+drug test
在 HN 中查看
defrost 2026/04/28 10:45
> 所以很难判断其中有多少是真正的酒驾，还是警察决定靠边停车（通常是黑人）

如果您查看（例如）澳大利亚道路交通执法统计数据，就会更容易；他们的规则与美国不同，有“权利”检查酒后驾车而无需编造理由，并通过漏斗路障进行操作，检查 大家 （或每隔一辆车等，取决于流速和呼吸检查速度）。

他们还“验证”任何司机都可以在监督下在车站提出挑战并获得“比路边更好”的测试（使用保存的样本进行血液测试以供法庭挑战等）。

> so it's very hard to tell how many of these are actual DUIs vs cops deciding to pull over a (usually black)

It's easier if you look at (say) Australian road traffic enforcement statistics; they have different rules than the US, have the "right" to check for DUI without having to make up a reason, and operate by funnelled road blocks that check everybody (or every second car, etc, depending on flow rates and breath check speeds).

They also 'verify' in the sense that any driver can challenge and get a "better than road side" test back at the station under supervision (blood tests with saved samples for court challenges, etc).

在 HN 中查看
esseph 2026/04/28 13:35
> 他们还“验证”任何驾驶员都可以在监督下在车站提出挑战并获得“比路边更好”的测试（使用保存的样本进行血液测试以供法庭挑战等）。

验血可以 不是 确定 THC 中毒。

> They also 'verify' in the sense that any driver can challenge and get a "better than road side" test back at the station under supervision (blood tests with saved samples for court challenges, etc).

Blood tests can not determine THC intoxication.

在 HN 中查看
defrost 2026/04/28 14:02
我没有看到我说的那部分。

DUI（在澳大利亚）指的是“酒后驾驶”，重点是阈值检测而不是损伤程度（尽管依赖于声称将两者关联的其他研究）。

在药物损害的情况下，“比路边拭子更好”的法庭质疑证据包括尿液、呼吸、头发、唾液或汗液样本。

血液样本（而不是呼气测试）通常用于检测酒精含量。

无论路边药物/酒精浓度拭子通过/失败，澳大利亚 RTA 仍然可以要求司机完成损伤测试；评估平衡、协调和整体行为。

在受到质疑时，这也可以作为法庭证据进一步评估；例如：他们对损伤的看法是因为（各种）医疗状况导致言语不清等。

I'm not seeing the part where I said that.

DUI (in Australia) refers to "Driving under influence" and focuses on threshold detection rather than degree of impairment (although relying upon other studies that allege to correlate the two).

In the case of drug impairment, "better than roadside swabs" evidence for court challenges includes samples of urine, breath, hair, saliva or sweat.

Blood samples, rather than breath tests, are typically for alcohol.

Independent of roadside pass / fail on swabs for drug / alcohol levels Australian RTA can still ask drivers to complete an impairment test; assessing balance, coordination, and overall behaviour.

On challenge, this is something that can also be further assessed for court evidence; eg: are their perceptions of impairment because (various) medical conditions that cause slurred words, etc.

在 HN 中查看
mckn1ght 2026/04/28 10:32
> 我一直试图向人们询问这个问题，但还没有得到满意的答案。 > 我的意思是我从来没有消费过，也永远不会消费，那么为什么你们的社会认为它可以接受呢？

鉴于后一种说法，我不确定您能否得到满意的答案。

我想说，作为一个在过去 15 年里很少喝酒的人，我喜欢它的两点：味道（我喜欢啤酒、葡萄酒、苏格兰威士忌、波本威士忌、杜松子酒、龙舌兰酒和各种利口酒）和微醉时的深邃感：与朋友交谈、现场音乐，很多事情都变得更加美好。

当然，这只是一种改变的感知状态，但生命的全部都是感知，所以它并不比任何其他精神状态更真实。

然而它也有很多缺点，很多人都被它带得太远了，以至于它曾经给他们带来的一点乐趣早已消失了。

我根本无法再应对哪怕是最轻微的宿醉，参加完聚会或喝完晚饭回家后，或者第二天早上，我会做一些我想做的事情，并且想要一个清醒的头脑。所以我停了下来。

现在，我偶尔会想喝啤酒，大约50%的时候是不含酒精的啤酒，酒精含量仍然很少，而且我仍然感觉有点醉。或者，如果我有一杯真正的啤酒，想再喝一杯，我会接着喝不含酒精的啤酒。

> I have been trying to ask people this but its havent gotten a satisfactory answer. > I mean I have never consumed and never will so why is it that your society finds it acceptable?

I’m not sure it will be possible for you to receive an answer that would satisfy, given that latter statement.

I’ll say as someone that rarely drinks for the past 15 years that I enjoy two things about it: the flavor (I like beers, wine, scotch, bourbon, gin, tequila and various liqueurs) and the sense of profundity you get when slightly inebriated: conversations with friends, live music, many things become enhanced.

Of course it’s only an altered state of perception but all of life is perception, so it’s no less real than any other mental state.

However it comes with a lot of downsides and many people get taken in way too far by it, to the point that what little enjoyment it might have given them at one time is long gone.

I simply couldn’t deal with the slightest hangover any more, I’d have things I’d want to do after getting home from a party or dinner with wine, or the next morning, and wanted a clear head. So I stopped.

Now, on the rare occasion I want a beer, about 50% of the time it’s a nonalcoholic one, which still has a small amount of alcohol, and I still feel slightly inebriated. Or if I have a real beer and want another to sip on, I’ll follow it up with nonalcoholic ones.

tejohnso 2026/04/28 10:05
我们对酒类缴纳额外税。没关系。人们付钱并消费。与汽油相同。看起来多贵并不重要。投诉率上升，但公路旅行仍在继续。

酒精对某些人来说尤其有害，但对其他人来说，它几乎总能带来更愉快的体验。我不认为那些人会试图说这对他们有好处，但他们会说他们喜欢它，这对他们的社交生活很重要，而且它并不比垃圾食品更糟糕。

We pay extra tax on alcohol. It doesn't matter. People pay it and consume. Same with gasoline. Doesn't seem to matter how expensive it gets. Rate of complaining goes up, but road trips continue.

Alcohol is especially harmful for some people, but for others it almost always leads to a more enjoyable experience. I don't think those people would try to say it's good for them but they will say they like it, it's important to their social life, and it's no worse than junk food.
